title: pycnanthemum aristatum var. hyssopifolium
intro: Pycnanthemum aristatum var. hyssopifolium, commonly known as mountain mint or hyssop-leaved mountain mint, is a variety of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to North America and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, and has been shown to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The leaves and flowers of Pycnanthemum aristatum var. hyssopifolium can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.
